data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: Apple utilizes a microservices architecture to ensure independent services can be scaled and developed separately.
data.technical_spec: Multiple lightweight services with distinct capabilities, communicating over HTTP/HTTPS with data usually serialized in JSON.

data.title: Cloud-Native Infrastructure
data.description: Infrastructure based on cloud technologies that can dynamically scale based on user demand.
data.technical_spec: Integration with public/private cloud providers, Kubernetes for orchestration.

data.title: Server-Side Rendering
data.description: Content is dynamically generated on the server to improve loading times and SEO performance.
data.technical_spec: Utilizes frameworks like Next.js for server-side rendering capabilities.

data.title: Load Balancing
data.description: Distributes incoming web traffic across multiple servers to ensure stability and performance.
data.technical_spec: Implemented using hardware or software-based solutions like Nginx or AWS ELB.

data.title: Continuous Integration/Continuous Deployment (CI/CD)
data.description: Automated pipeline for testing and deploying code changes promptly and efficiently.
data.technical_spec: Tools like Jenkins or CircleCI used for CI/CD pipelines.
